title: Uncompiled vs compiled — normal dev, release-day wave convert
statement: Uncompiled runners execute at native interpreter speed during development. No artificial slowdown. Wave conversion to the single plane is a release-day step via Compile mode.

launch_format.statement: .launch is an organized field — inspect files, trim excess, run interpreter or BSP reuse; never compile on launch.

python_interpreter.statement: Python BSP — staged GrokVM driver from exec-plane, same parity as native BSP reuse

singular_field_plane.statement: Compile mode / QUEEN_LAUNCH_SINGULAR_FIELD=1 — wave-convert to plane when BSP miss.

combinatorics.statement: Hold all field combinatorics — speed cap and headroom for commonly used patterns beyond current defaults

truth_blocks.free_meld: bigger truth blocks and more of them → fuse without compile gate

iron_plate.statement: Identical C/C++ kernels convert to Python on the iron plate — dev runs the field face; exec reuses native BSP. Always breaks down to assembly, entropy, field.

release_compile.trigger: Compile mode — Queen Files right-click on compileable sources

combinatronics.statement: Release compile runs optimal combinatronics before wave-convert; plane.json carries stamped receipt.

python_speedups.statement: Python runs interpreter lane — no compile on launch; GrokVM + field_opt profile active

optimization_backlog.note: gpy-16 field_native + PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE + interpreter BSP wired

optimization_backlog.target: pythong → g16 wave convert on release Compile mode
g16-ld C++ stdlib meld for belt_2 CMake plane
